Hey, what's going on everyone? Okay, so I've had, I mean, I'll tell you a little story. So Travis Jones was my first business mentor way back when I was 23 and I did it before I had a gym. For some reason I sought out mentors before I opened the gym so I could open it up right. That for some reason made sense to me, which it did make sense. So anyways, he was talking about sales stuff and I never sold anything before and he was like, hey man, you want to know the secret to sales? And I was like, yes, I don't like the, what's the secret? So excited. He was like, this guy's going to be the secret to sales. And he said, get people on offer, they can't refuse. He's like, get people on offer that they'd fit stupid to say no to. And for some reason that just like really resonated with me because it was just like, make your job easier as a salesman by giving better offers. So I was like, huh, that's cool. And so that always stuck with me. And so you'll notice, you know, if you've been working with me for a while that I always like making great offers because I think offered like making irresistible offers is what's key to getting people to take action and ultimately make sales and make people happy. So, you know, Russell jokes about he's like, I was the best sales guy. I know it's, it's, I really don't think that I'm that kind of like, I mean, I think I'm using it, but I think it really comes down to making sure that you have offers that are irresistible because it just makes your job a lot easier. Right. And so like, I would like to have easier jobs selling people. So it's like, how can I make it easier for myself? Give them better offers. Cool. So I people offers that people would feel stupid to say no to. So what is like, what are the components of an irresistible offer? Okay. And so these are the three components that I had and all the offers that I'll give people. So first is a stack, second is scarcity and then third is urgency. When we have all three things start to become your system. So I'll talk about the abandoned wholesale and then I'll make it relevant with like our mastermind sale that I just did, just to give you two examples. So with the abandoned wholesale that we do at the halfway point of the challenge, you want to stack it. So there your stack is you're going to get your six weeks free no matter what. Okay. You're going to be able to, we're going to weave the initiation fee. All right. And you're going to be able to stay at a lower price for life. Those are three, that's a big stack, right? It's a big stack. And if you want, you can add even more things to that stack same like and on top of that, we're going to give you a one on one nutrition oriented, you know, a consultation for like life after the challenge and then give you a more sustainable plan that will like to eat the things that you want to eat and still lose weight and keep all the progress that we've made and ultimately keep it off. Right? So like you won't like this was great. You got great results. We're happy for you. But like to make this like what you really want, you want to lose this way for life, right? And then say, yes, cool. So like, you're probably not going to eat this way forever, right? And then say, no, like, right. So like we need to get you on that plan and understanding how to do this as soon as possible. Otherwise you could risk fear of loss. You could risk losing all the progress that you've made and how long you've worked. Right? And so people will be like, man, I really need that. So you want to give them like it's basically an upsell, but you want to give them with the stack something that they don't have that they need. All right. So it's especially it's like if you give them a solution, there, no matter what solution you have, you're going to give partial solutions because like you can't give answers to the universe. Right? So for example, so that's that would be within the context of the challenge. For me, right? I can offer a gym lunch and that can be the first thing. And then everyone's like, Oh, my gosh, that's amazing. And then I could easily just say, awesome. Well, do you want to know what the next next offer is so that you can rotate offers so that you don't saturate your market with this one? Or do you want the newer ads that you can use to do the gym lunch again and have the same effectiveness as you did the first time? Whatever that thing is like, there's like, do you want to have all the retention strategies so that you can keep all of these people and keep 100% of them instead of just half, right? Like each of those things would be something like, I have this thing, but I totally need that. And so it's being able to know what the next thing is going to need to be like, so even and then from a larger strategic standpoint, I could say, Hey guys, I know I've taught you how to get people in your door and fill your gym up. But but how you do that without taking all your time, right? I got you money. That's cool. But you're now you're working yourself to death. So like, want me to show you how to keep that same income that inflow and then be able to stay at home every night with your kids? So it's like knowing what that next product, like the nice thing is that every problem or every like every problem that you solve for someone is going to open up another problem. And so like problems don't go away. They just change. They evolve. Like what happens with like when you're empty, you're empty and you have a problem with your own clients. When you're full, you have a problem because you have, you know, issues with classes being too full. Like there's always problems that's just figuring out what the next one's going to be for your prospecting and solving ahead of time and then offering you once they've reached that next point, right? So anyways, I know it's long-winded, but it's important. So that's the step. Okay. Now, a really, really important example that I want to cover is splintering your offers. And why that's important is so let's say so we had a cookbook, right? And so I had a cookbook and the cookbook, you know, that's cool. But as soon as we splintered it and changed it from a cookbook to bombast breakfasts, lick your fingers, lunches, delectable dinners, and then like the sexy shaped guide or whatever, sexy back shake guide. We took one thing and made it four. And so I'd be like, listen, if you act today, I used to say like, you're going to get a cookbook too. They'd be like, that's cool. But I'm like, if you act today, not only are you going to get your six weeks free, you're going to get no initiation fee, you're going to get a lifetime lower membership. We're also going to throw in our bombast breakfasts, our delectable dinners, our lick your fingers, lunches, and our sexy back shake guide and a nutrition consultation that will allow you to keep these, keep these results for life by eating the foods that you love. You say that to someone, they're like, yeah, I'm in. They're like, sign me up because that's an amazing offer. Okay. So what I did was I took one thing and I splintered it into four or five different things. So it just sounds bigger because I mean, if I have a thousand page cookbook, I can say cookbook, but it's like way cooler if you can splinter it off into different pieces. Cool. So hopefully that makes sense. All right. So that's the step. Scarcity. So a lot of people say scarcity and urgency, but like you have to know like, so scarcity is, there's only X amount of these things and the first X amount to get this thing. And then that's it. Right. So it's how many spots, how many people we're taking on. Okay. That's scarcity. All right. Urgency is you have to act by this time, by tomorrow, by right now. Right. And so you take that stack, right? You get this, this, this, this, this, this and this. And I can only do that off of the first 10 people. And you got to let me know right now. That is an irresistible offer. All right. So that's the anatomy, the irresistible offer. So whenever you're making offers for people, try to make sure that you have those three components in them. Because when you do that, people will buy from you. And then you don't have to feel like you're selling, you're just making irresistible offers. And then you'll sell them. And then it'll be a lot easier and just more fun. So, hope that was useful for you. Have an awesome, awesome day. Bye.
